content:
# Recommendations for the next plan-agent iteration (iter-129)

## CRITICAL вЂ” must-fix-this-iter from review-phase audits

### CRITICAL #1 вЂ” Signature relaxation on `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.lieAlgebra`

`lean-vs-blueprint-checker-cotangent-grpobj-review128` flags a **must-fix-this-iter** signature mismatch: the iter-128 prover landed `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 G.hom]` on `lieAlgebra` (hardcoded rel-dim-1), but the blueprint chapter `lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra` prose is dimension-agnostic ("smooth ... group scheme") AND the downstream consumer `thm:rigidity_over_kbar` applies `lieAlgebra` to an abelian variety `A` of arbitrary relative dimension `g := dim A`, which **cannot** instantiate the rel-dim-1 form.

Action: dispatch a `refactor` lane on `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean` relaxing the signature to one of:

- (a) `[Smooth G.hom]` вЂ” matches the chapter prose exactly. The body never touches the smoothness hypothesis (it is purely categorical), so the relaxation is signature-only and cannot break the body.
- (b) `{n : в„•} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]` вЂ” keeps a relative-dimension parameter exposed so the iter-129+ rank lemma `lieAlgebra_finrank_eq_dim` can share `n` directly via the same instance.

Recommended pick: **(b)**, because the iter-129+ rank-lemma chapter (`lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank`) explicitly uses `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]` as the rank-`n` parameter source. Sharing the `n` binder between the two declarations avoids a `letI` dance in the rank-lemma proof.

Estimate: 1-line signature edit + zero cascading consumer fixes (no in-tree consumers exist yet). 1 iter / < 10 LOC.

### CRITICAL #2 вЂ” Docstring / name vs body inconsistency on `lieAlgebra`

`lean-auditor-review128` flags a **must-fix-this-iter** body-vs-docstring mismatch on the SAME file: the opening docstring (`Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:58вЂ“60`) asserts the result is the *k-linear dual* of `О·_G^* О©_{G/k}` (i.e.\ the Lie algebra `рќ”¤` = tangent space at identity), but the body вЂ” and the second Status docstring at `:79вЂ“82`, and the inline comment at `:99вЂ“101` вЂ” returns `О·_G^* О©_{G/k}` itself (the cotangent space `рќ”¤^в€Ё`, no dualisation step). The chapter's encoding note (`RigidityKbar.tex:102`) authorises either convention, so the body's choice is not a chapter violation, but the file's docstrings disagree with each other and with the name `lieAlgebra`.

Action вЂ” pick ONE and apply uniformly:

- **(a) Keep body, rename + fix docstring**: rename `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.lieAlgebra` в†’ `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentAtIdentity` (or similar); rewrite the opening docstring to drop "$k$-linear dual" and say "cotangent space `рќ”¤^в€Ё = О·_G^* О©_{G/k}` at the identity section"; update the chapter `\lean{...}` hint at `RigidityKbar.tex:94` and `Jacobian.tex` cross-references accordingly.
- **(b) Keep name + opening docstring, wrap body**: change the body to apply a `ModuleCat`-level $k$-linear dual (`ModuleCat.dual` or equivalent) to the current return value; rewrite the Status docstring to say "Lie algebra = dual of cotangent space, returned as a k-module" so all docstrings agree.

Recommended pick: **(a)** because (i) the body is mathematically correct *as written* (it produces a canonical fg-free `k`-module attached to the identity, which is what the rigidity argument consumes), and (ii) the chapter's existing prose already names the cotangent presheaf pullback `рќ”¤^\vee`, so the rename aligns with the chapter's primary symbol. **(b)** doubles the body's work and forces an early choice of `ModuleCat.dual` infrastructure when the rigidity argument has no need for the dual.

Estimate: 1-line rename + 5вЂ“10 LOC of docstring rewrite + 1-line chapter `\lean{...}` update + cross-reference sweep across `Jacobian.tex`. 1 iter / < 50 LOC.

### CRITICAL #3 вЂ” Bundle CRITICAL #1 + #2 into a single iter-129 refactor lane

Both fixes touch the same file (`Cotangent/GrpObj.lean`) and the same chapter (`RigidityKbar.tex`); both are tiny. Bundle them as a single refactor-lane directive (`refactor-cotangent-grpobj-signature-and-rename-iter129`) to land them together. Net delta: 1 file edit (signature + docstring + rename), 1 chapter edit (`\lean{...}` + `% NOTE:` strip), 1 cross-reference sweep. Zero new sorries. Should compile in 1 iter.

After this lane lands, the `% NOTE:` block this iter added immediately before `lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra` in `RigidityKbar.tex` should be stripped вЂ” both items it flags will be resolved.

## HIGH вЂ” chapter adequacy for iter-129+ rank-lemma prover lane

`lean-vs-blueprint-checker-cotangent-grpobj-review128` flags a **major** blueprint adequacy gap:

The chapter's `\begin{proof}` of `lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra` (RigidityKbar.tex:105вЂ“109) sketches only the `рќ”Є/рќ”ЄВІ`-at-identity stalk-side route via `IsRegularLocalRing.cotangentSpace`. But the Lean body actually computed `О“(G, О©_{G/k}) вЉ—_{О“(G)} k` (evaluate-at-top FIRST, extend-scalars SECOND). These are NOT the same operation on a general presheaf вЂ” they coincide for **proper geometrically irreducible** schemes (where `О“(G, O_G) = k`), but the equivalence requires the non-trivial **translation-invariance theorem** (global differentials on a group scheme are translation-invariant, hence canonically `в‰…` cotangent at identity).

For iter-128 (just landing the definition), this is fine вЂ” both presentations produce a canonically-defined `ModuleCat k`. But the iter-129+ rank lemma `lieAlgebra_finrank_eq_dim` will need to prove `Module.finrank k (lieAlgebra G) = n` for **this specific term** (the evaluate-first construction), and the chapter's prose route via `IsRegularLocalRing.cotangentSpace` will not directly apply.

Action: dispatch a `blueprint-writer` on `RigidityKbar.tex` to:

- (a) Add a paragraph after the existing `\begin{proof}` of `lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra` (or a new helper-lemma block) **identifying the canonical iso between the evaluate-first and stalk-side presentations** for the proper-geom-irred case, naming the load-bearing Mathlib pieces (translation invariance of global differentials on a group scheme вЂ” likely a project-internal helper, since Mathlib `b80f227` does not carry a packaged "translation-invariant differentials on group scheme" result; cf. `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Smooth.translationStalkIso` if it exists, otherwise admit as a project Mathlib-gap candidate alongside piece (iii)).
- (b) Pin the iter-129+ Lean signature for `lieAlgebra_finrank_eq_dim` so the next prover lane has a fully-specified target (the chapter's existing line 116 has `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]` correctly, but the Lean encoding note at line 120 should also explicitly name the bridge lemma `lieAlgebra` will go through).

Estimate: ~40-60 lines of blueprint prose; 1 iter; no Lean changes.

## HIGH вЂ” stale file header in `Jacobian.lean`

`lean-auditor-review128` flags `Jacobian.lean:14вЂ“19`: the file header still claims `nonempty_jacobianWitness` is "the **single remaining mathematical sorry** of the Phase-C scaffolding", which was true pre-iter-127 but is now stale вЂ” `genusZeroWitness` (iter-127 scaffold, body `sorry` at L178) is a SECOND sorry-bodied declaration in the same file.

Action: bundle into the iter-129 refactor lane (CRITICAL #3) or land as a tiny separate edit. Rewrite the file header to acknowledge both `genusZeroWitness` (iter-138+ closure) and `nonempty_jacobianWitness` (M2+M3 packaging) as the two open sorries, OR rephrase the header to be progress-tracking-agnostic.

Estimate: 5-line edit.

## MEDIUM вЂ” `genusZeroWitness` orphaned, expected per closure plan

`lean-auditor-review128` flags as **minor**: `genusZeroWitness` (`Jacobian.lean:174вЂ“178`) has zero in-tree consumers (only the blueprint `\lean{...}` hint in `Jacobian.tex`). This is expected per the iter-138+ closure plan вЂ” `genusZeroWitness` is wired into `nonempty_jacobianWitness` only after M2.a (`rigidity_over_kbar`) body closes. No iter-129 action required, but worth tracking so iter-129 plan-agent does not flag it again unnecessarily.

## STRATEGIC вЂ” iter-128 META-PATTERN TRIPWIRE outcome resolves META-PATTERN to CONVERGING

Per `progress-critic-iter128`: "a single-`sorry`-closure is enough to flip META-PATTERN toward CONVERGING; two unresolved sorries flips it to STUCK and forces user escalation." The iter-128 prover lane closed **the** new sorry on `lieAlgebra` (no PARTIAL, no INCOMPLETE; clean body close, kernel-axioms-only). The META-PATTERN flips to **CONVERGING**. The iter-129 fallback rule (codified in iter-128 plan.md per the progress-critic's Q3) does NOT trigger вЂ” its trigger condition was "iter-128 prover returns INCOMPLETE or PARTIAL-with-`lieAlgebra`-still-`sorry`", and neither holds.

iter-129 is therefore free to proceed with normal planning: the iter-129 work is the fix-up refactor lane (CRITICAL #3 above) plus the chapter-adequacy writer pass (HIGH above). Both are 1-iter / small-LOC tasks; iter-129 may also fit in an additional prover lane on a piece (i.b) or (ii) sub-piece if appetite allows. Resist over-bundling вЂ” the iter-128 success was partly the strategy-critic's iter-128 alternative #1 re-scoping to "definition only" rather than "definition + rank lemma". Honor that pattern.

## STRATEGIC вЂ” chapter encoding note's "prover discretion" latitude paid off

The chapter `lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra` encoding note at `RigidityKbar.tex:102` explicitly authorised either dualisation choice ("Whether to dualise inside the declaration (returning $\mathfrak g$) or to leave it as $\mathfrak g^\vee$ ... is at the prover's discretion"). The prover used this latitude to land the simpler (non-dualised, cotangent-side) body, which compiled and verified with no axioms. The cost of the latitude was the docstring inconsistency above вЂ” the prover's commit took advantage of the latitude in the body but the opening docstring was carried over from a scaffold that anticipated the dualised form. **Lesson for future scaffolding refactors**: when the chapter authorises latitude, the SCAFFOLD docstrings should either pick one and commit (preferable) OR explicitly acknowledge the latitude so the prover knows to align the opening docstring with whichever choice they make in the body.

## BACKLOG (deferred from iter-128, still relevant)

The iter-127 review's recommendations carry forward where not yet addressed:

- Rename `rigidity_over_kbar` в†’ `rigidity_over_k` to match the iter-127 over-k commitment (small refactor; defer unless iter-129 has spare capacity).
- Blueprint cleanup pass on `Jacobian.tex` В§ C.2.aвЂ“C.2.e over-`kМ„` prose, `AbelJacobi.tex`, `Rigidity.tex В§ "Use in the project"` (в„™В№_{kМ„} mentions), and orphan-chapter deletion (`Modules_Monoidal.tex`, `Picard_LineBundle.tex`, `Picard_Functor.tex`, `Picard_FunctorAb.tex` per `blueprint-reviewer-iter128`'s cross-chapter notes).
- iter-129+ prover lane on piece (i.b) (`AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.mulRight_globalises_cotangent`, functorial shear iso) is now unblocked by the iter-128 piece (i.a) close вЂ” this is the natural next prover target after the CRITICAL #3 fix-up lands. Per the chapter's `lem:GrpObj_mulRight_globalises` prose, the construction is pure category theory (Mathlib `GrpObj.mul`, `GrpObj.inv`, `GrpObj.one` API) plus the binary-product projection identities, so it does not depend on the iter-129 chapter-adequacy bridge work (that gates the RANK lemma, not the shear-iso lemma).
